description:
A cloud-architect colleague handed you a compressed bundle that contains the raw logs produced during the first rehearsal of a service-migration to Kubernetes.  
    The bundle has already been decompressed for you in:

        /home/user/cloud_migration/

    Inside that directory you will find the file

        /home/user/cloud_migration/full_system.log

    Goal  
    ----  
    1. From that log you must extract ONLY the lines that  
       • have a severity tag of **[ERROR]** or **[CRITICAL]** **AND**  
       • belong to the **db-service** component (the component name appears in the third bracket pair).  

    2. Write all matching lines, in their original order and **without any additional characters or blank lines**, to

        /home/user/cloud_migration/alerts/critical_db.log

       If the directory **/home/user/cloud_migration/alerts/** does not yet exist, create it first.

    3. Create a very small statistics file named

        /home/user/cloud_migration/alerts/critical_db_summary.txt

       containing exactly three lines **in the order given below**, with no leading or trailing spaces:

            ERROR_COUNT=<integer>
            CRITICAL_COUNT=<integer>
            TOTAL=<integer>

       • ERROR_COUNT  – number of filtered lines containing “[ERROR]”  
       • CRITICAL_COUNT – number of filtered lines containing “[CRITICAL]”  
       • TOTAL – the sum of the previous two values  

       Example of the exact required format (values will differ):

            ERROR_COUNT=7
            CRITICAL_COUNT=2
            TOTAL=9

    Verification expectations  
    -------------------------  
    Automated tests will assert all of the following:

    a) The file /home/user/cloud_migration/alerts/critical_db.log exists.  
    b) It contains ONLY the correct db-service lines with severities ERROR or CRITICAL and preserves the original order present in full_system.log.  
    c) The file /home/user/cloud_migration/alerts/critical_db_summary.txt exists and matches the key=value format shown above, with integer counts that correspond exactly to the content of critical_db.log.  
    d) No superfluous files are created inside /home/user/cloud_migration/alerts/.  

    You may use any standard CLI tools (grep, awk, sed, wc, etc.).  
    Typical solution length: 3-4 commands.
truth:
Initial state before the agent starts
    ------------------------------------
    • Directory /home/user/cloud_migration/ exists and is writable by the current user.  
    • The subdirectory /home/user/cloud_migration/alerts/ does NOT exist yet.  
    • File /home/user/cloud_migration/full_system.log contains exactly the 10 lines shown below (including the final newline at the end of the file):

[2023-10-01 08:10:16] [INFO] [auth-service] User login successful userId=23
[2023-10-01 08:10:18] [WARN] [gateway] High latency detected route=/api/payments
[2023-10-01 08:10:20] [ERROR] [db-service] Connection timeout to primary replica
[2023-10-01 08:11:05] [INFO] [billing-service] Invoice generated invoiceId=9821
[2023-10-01 08:12:45] [CRITICAL] [db-service] Data corruption detected segment=alpha
[2023-10-01 08:13:02] [ERROR] [cache-service] Redis cluster unreachable
[2023-10-01 08:14:33] [INFO] [db-service] Background vacuum completed table=orders
[2023-10-01 08:15:14] [WARN] [auth-service] Suspicious login attempt userId=88
[2023-10-01 08:16:29] [ERROR] [db-service] Failed to apply migration file V23__add_index.sql
[2023-10-01 08:17:42] [INFO] [gateway] Deployment finished version=1.3.4

    Expected final state after a correct solution
    ---------------------------------------------
    1. Directory /home/user/cloud_migration/alerts/ exists.  

    2. File /home/user/cloud_migration/alerts/critical_db.log exists and its exact contents are:

[2023-10-01 08:10:20] [ERROR] [db-service] Connection timeout to primary replica
[2023-10-01 08:12:45] [CRITICAL] [db-service] Data corruption detected segment=alpha
[2023-10-01 08:16:29] [ERROR] [db-service] Failed to apply migration file V23__add_index.sql

    The file ends with a single newline (UNIX line ending).

    3. File /home/user/cloud_migration/alerts/critical_db_summary.txt exists and its exact contents are:

ERROR_COUNT=2
CRITICAL_COUNT=1
TOTAL=3

    It also ends with a trailing newline.

    4. No other files are present inside /home/user/cloud_migration/alerts/.
name: task_000000_5e01077c
